name: Random Names Age Analysis Workflow
description: Generate random names with ages, analyze by age decade, and save results

tasks.name: Generate Random Names CSV
tasks.description: You MUST call python_exec to generate a CSV string containing 50 random names with ages between 18-65. Use Python's random module to generate random names (create random combinations from lists of first/last names or use simple name generation). Generate complete CSV content with header 'name,age' and 50 data rows. Then you MUST call file_write to save the CSV content to 'people.csv'. Output format: 'CSV file created at people.csv with 50 entries'.
tasks.success_criteria: Output confirms CSV file 'people.csv' was written and contains 50 rows with name and age columns

tasks.name: Analyze Age Groups
tasks.description: You MUST call file_read to read 'people.csv' created in task_001. Then you MUST call python_exec to parse the CSV and group people by age decade: teens (18-19), twenties (20-29), thirties (30-39), forties (40-49), fifties (50-59), sixties (60-65). Count how many people are in each group. Output the COMPLETE analysis results verbatim showing each age group name and its count. Do NOT summarize as 'analysis complete' — output the actual age group names and counts in a structured text format ready to be saved to a file. Example output format: 'Teens (18-19): 5\nTwenties (20-29): 12\n...'
tasks.success_criteria: Output contains specific counts for each age decade (teens, twenties, thirties, forties, fifties, sixties) with exact numbers formatted as readable text

tasks.name: Save Analysis Results
tasks.description: You MUST call file_write to save the age group analysis from task_002's output to the file 'age_groups.txt'. Use the exact analysis text from task_002 that contains the age decade counts. Output: 'Analysis saved to age_groups.txt'.

metadata.goal: Write a Python script that generates 50 random names with ages (18-65) as a CSV file. Then read the CSV, group by age decade (teens, twenties, thirties, etc.), and count how many people are in each group. Save the analysis as age_groups.txt.
